survivors_flagged.one_line: Yellow Wood $30/sh bid rejected as too low (June 2026); Brandes 14.4% stake, feminine-care divested, board engaged — live take-private/auction setup.

$22B all-stock merger with Corebridge announced Mar 2026; EQH holders get ~49%, close targeted year-end 2026 pending shareholder/regulatory approvals (legal scrutiny live) — dated stock-for-stock arb.
Pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015 Ph1 mono data H1 2026 (imminent) + ERAS-4001 H2 2026; real dated binaries but early data already re-rated, clinical-readout not classic special-sit.
ARCHIMED buyout $3.16 cash + CVR (up to $100M milestones); shareholder vote Jul 8 2026, close Q3 2026 — clean merger-arb/CVR.
EVO301 atopic-derm Ph2a (H1'26) + EVO756 AD Ph2b (Q3'26) readouts pending, but CSU Ph2b just FAILED 6/29/26 — dents the thesis; surviving dated binaries only.
EDG-7500 CIRRUS-HCM Part D Ph2 data Q2'26 (unfired); $2.65B Servier asset sale largely re-rated, transforms it to a cardio pure-play funded through approval.
DURAVYU pivotal Ph3 wet-AMD: LUGANO topline mid-2026, LUCIA shortly after, fully enrolled (>900pts), 3rd consecutive positive DSMC — clean dated binary, the core de-risking event.
4D-150 wet-AMD: PRISM 2yr Phase 2b mid-2026 + SPECTRA H2-2026 readouts are real dated binaries, but pivotal 4FRONT topline slips to H1-2027 — asymmetry not yet imminent.
Long Ridge sale to MARA (~$1.52B) signed Apr 30 2026, expected close Q3 2026 with >$300M net proceeds to repay corporate debt — a real dated deleveraging milestone with the close still forward.

verified.catalyst: Edgewell's board rejected Yellow Wood Partners' unsolicited ~$30/share take-private bid as too low, leaving a live but largely-priced take-private/auction setup with Brandes holding 14.4%.
verified.dated_milestone: Bid rejected June 22-23, 2026; no announced strategic-review or auction process and no dated next step — next read is Q3 FY2026 earnings (~early Aug 2026) or any sweetened/competing bid.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Yellow Wood Partners) + G2 (concrete dated $30/sh rejected bid)
verified.primary_source: Bloomberg "Schick Razor Maker Edgewell's Shares Leap on Rejected Offer" (2026-06-23) + moomoo/MT Newswires market chatter (2026-06-22); Brandes 13G/A via StockTitan (as of 2026-03-31); FMP quote EPC $27.63 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real and specific — Yellow Wood Partners (named PE buyer, G1) made an unsolicited ~$30/share offer that Edgewell's board rejected as too low on June 22-23, 2026 (Bloomberg, MT Newswires), with Brandes a 14.4% holder (13G/A, but PASSIVE not an activist 13D, so the 'board engaged/activist' framing is overstated). However the event has substantially fired: EPC surged ~18% on the news to ~$27.63, now only ~8% below the rejected bid and well above its ~$21 50-day / ~$20 200-day averages, so the market already prices a high deal probability. There is no announced formal sale process, strategic review, raised bid, or competing bidder as of late June 2026 — the forward leg lacks a dated, concrete commitment — so it gates to WATCH with compressed edge rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: EQH/Corebridge all-stock merger of equals into "New Equitable" — simultaneous shareholder votes set for July 30, 2026, targeting a year-end 2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder votes (both companies) July 30, 2026; target close year-end 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (EQH common; ratio 1.55516 EQH : 1 New Equitable share; CRBG 1.000:1)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Corebridge/ex-AIG) + G2 (signed agreement, fixed exchange ratios, board-approved) PASS; G3 FAILS — no specific currently-unpriced figure (no cash premium; ratio spread already tight).
verified.edge: L — stock-for-stock merger of EQUALS with no takeout premium. EQH $44.025 vs implied 1.55516 x CRBG $28.645 = ~$44.55 → only ~1.2% gross spread to a ~6-month close (~2.4% annualized). HSR antitrust period already expired June 5, 2026, so the deal is largely de-risked and priced; remaining risk is state insurance approvals + vote, not upside.
verified.primary_source: Businesswire merger announcement 2026-03-25; DEFM14A merger proxy (vote set July 30, 2026); MLex 2026-06 (HSR waiting period expired June 5, 2026); Rowley Law PLLC investigation notice (2026).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, named, signed and dated: a $22B all-stock merger of EQH and Corebridge (ex-AIG) into "New Equitable," with simultaneous shareholder votes July 30, 2026 and a year-end-2026 target close — G1 and G2 pass. But it fails the unpriced-edge test (G3): this is a merger of equals with fixed exchange ratios and NO cash premium, and at EQH $44.025 vs the implied ~$44.55 (1.55516 x CRBG $28.645) the ratio spread is only ~1.2% gross / ~2.4% annualized — a thin, fully-priced arb. US antitrust (HSR) already cleared June 5, 2026, so the deal is largely de-risked; the live "legal scrutiny" is routine plaintiff-firm (Rowley Law) M&A boilerplate, not a blocker. Net: a genuine dated forward event but low-edge and near-priced → WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: ERAS-4001 (pan-KRAS) BOREALIS-1 initial Phase 1 monotherapy data in H2 2026 is the next dated binary, but the lead ERAS-0015 pan-RAS readout already fired and re-rated the stock.
verified.dated_milestone: ERAS-4001 BOREALIS-1 initial Ph1 monotherapy data — H2 2026; ERAS-0015 monotherapy-expansion + combination dose-escalation data — H1 2027
verified.bloom_gates: none cleanly met — G3 fails (no specific currently-unpriced figure; a Ph1 dose-escalation readout is probabilistic, not a quantified value gap); G1/G2 N/A (no counterparty or contractual commitment)
verified.edge: L — lead-asset data already fired (Apr 27 2026) and the name re-rated from ~$1.24 to ~$17 (mktcap ~$5.3B, 200d avg $8.89); remaining ERAS-4001 H2-2026 readout is early-stage Ph1 mono with no quantified unpriced bar.
verified.primary_source: Erasca PR "Positive Preliminary Phase 1 Dose Escalation Data for ERAS-0015" (Apr 27 2026) + Q1-2026 results (May 11 2026, ERAS-4001 data H2 2026 / ERAS-0015 expansion data H1 2027) + FMP quote (Jun 29 2026, $17.06)
verified.analysis: ERAS-0015's initial Phase 1 monotherapy dose-escalation data (the H1-2026 "imminent" binary the triage flagged) already read out positively on April 27, 2026 and the stock re-rated violently — ~$1.24 52-week low to $17.06 now, ~$5.3B market cap, well above its $8.89 200-day average — so that catalyst is fired and priced, not forward. The only un-fired dated binary left is ERAS-4001/BOREALIS-1 initial Ph1 monotherapy data in H2 2026, but as an early dose-escalation safety/PK/efficacy readout it has no specific currently-unpriced figure, named counterparty, or contractual commitment, so it fails the Bloom special-sit gates. This is a probabilistic clinical-readout name where the value-driving catalyst has already triggered — keep on WATCH for the H2-2026 ERAS-4001 print, but it does not qualify as an actionable un-fired special situation.

verified.catalyst: ARCHIMed (Essence Parent) all-cash buyout at $3.16/share plus a non-tradeable CVR worth up to $100M ($40M bempedoic-acid + $60M enbumyst U.S.-sales milestones); stockholder vote July 8, 2026, expected close Q3 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Special stockholder meeting / merger vote July 8, 2026 (8:00 a.m. ET); close Q3 2026
verified.instrument: equity (common); CVR is non-transferable, not separately tradeable
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty ARCHIMED/Essence Parent) + G2 (concrete $3.16 cash + CVR commitment in signed merger agreement)
verified.edge: L — ESPR last at $3.16 = exactly the cash consideration, so the arb spread is fully collapsed; both HSR (expired Jun 15) and German Bundeskartellamt (cleared Jun 2) antitrust gates already passed, leaving only the Jul 8 vote with the deal essentially fully priced. Residual CVR (~$0.48/sh gross) is a deeply-discounted binary on multi-year sales milestones, non-tradeable — not an unpriced dated catalyst (fails G3). Asymmetry is negative: trivial upside vs. drop toward ~$2 pre-deal level if vote fails.
verified.primary_source: Esperion DEFM14A / 8-K (SEC, filed Jun 2026) + TradingView/StockTitan: HSR waiting period expired Jun 15, 2026 and Bundeskartellamt cleared Jun 2, 2026; vote set Jul 8, 2026. Price $3.16 via FMP quote (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The $1.1B ARCHIMED buyout is real, signed, and dated (vote Jul 8, 2026; close Q3 2026), but the cash leg is fired: ESPR trades at exactly $3.16, equal to the cash consideration, and both U.S. (HSR, expired Jun 15) and German (Bundeskartellamt, cleared Jun 2) antitrust approvals are already in hand, so there is no remaining merger-arb spread. The only forward optionality is the non-tradeable CVR (up to $100M on bempedoic-acid/enbumyst U.S.-sales thresholds), which is a low-probability binary tail discounted near zero by the market — not a currently-unpriced, near-dated catalyst, so it fails the strict G3 test. Net: a clean but spread-collapsed merger with negative asymmetry into the vote, not an actionable catalyst; WATCH at most, score capped low.

verified.catalyst: EVO756 (oral MRGPRX2 antagonist) Phase 2b top-line readout in moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis, the surviving dated binary after the same molecule's CSU Phase 2b just missed its primary endpoint on 6/29/26.
verified.dated_milestone: EVO756 atopic dermatitis Phase 2b top-line data "on track for 3Q26" (per 6/29/26 release); next date Q3 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 — a specific, currently-unpriced Phase 2b efficacy readout (UAS7-analog/EASI in AD) on a dated near-term timeline; not a named-counterparty (G1) or signed-commitment (G2) event.
verified.edge: M/L — dated forward binary that is un-fired and the stock just crashed 34% (to $16.50, MC ~$234M, vs 50/200-day ~$22-23), so the AD readout is not yet re-rated; but the CSU Phase 2b just failed on the IDENTICAL molecule (EVO756), so mechanistic read-through into AD is materially impaired (the bear case is that MRGPRX2 antagonism simply doesn't deliver clinical efficacy), capping conviction. Cash through 2028 reduces financing-overhang risk into the print.
verified.primary_source: Evommune 6/29/26 BusinessWire/8-K (EVO756 CSU Ph2b top-line miss) + stocktitan 8-K summary quoting "Phase 2b top-line data from EVO756 in CSU and AD on track for June 2026 and 3Q26, respectively"; FMP quote EVMN $16.50, -34.5% on 6/29/26.
verified.analysis: Triage flag was garbled: EVO301 Ph2a in AD already read out POSITIVE (2/10/26, fired/re-rated) and the 6/29/26 failure was EVO756's CSU Phase 2b (UAS7 missed at 12 weeks across all doses; CSU development discontinued), not a separate program. The one genuinely surviving dated forward binary is EVO756 Phase 2b top-line in atopic dermatitis, which the same June 29 release reaffirmed as "on track for 3Q26"; EVO301's AD Phase 2b is only a mid-2027 start and EVO756 migraine topline is 2027 (too distant). It qualifies (dated, forward, unpriced post-34% crash, micro-cap binary, non-macro), but because it is the same molecule that just failed the related CSU indication, read-through is impaired and it stays WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: The flagged catalyst — EDG-7500 CIRRUS-HCM Part D Phase 2 12-week data — already FIRED positive on 2026-06-16, and the $2.65B Servier sevasemten sale is announced and largely re-rated, leaving only an operational Phase 3 start as a forward event.
verified.dated_milestone: Phase 3 EDG-7500 HCM program initiation targeted Q4 2026 (operational start, not a data readout); Servier sale close expected Q3 2026 (value already in price).
verified.bloom_gates: None pass cleanly. G1 (named counterparty Servier) and G2 (concrete $1.55B upfront + up to $1.1B milestone commitment) exist but are already announced and re-rated, not forward/unpriced. No G3 unpriced figure remains.
verified.edge: L — the primary thesis is stale: the Part D Phase 2 readout fired positive 2026-06-16 and the Servier deal is public; stock at $40.82 vs 52-wk low $12.30 (3.3x), near 52-wk high $43.93 and ~18% above 50-DMA $34.48, indicating both catalysts are substantially priced. Remaining Phase 3-start is operational, not a discrete value-unlock.
verified.primary_source: Edgewise PRNewswire, "Positive Top-Line Data from 12-Week Phase 2 CIRRUS-HCM" (2026-06-16); Edgewise/Servier sevasemten sale announcement (up to $2.65B); FMP quote EWTX $40.82, 52-wk $12.30-$43.93 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: Triage flagged Part D Ph2 data as "unfired," but it released positive top-line on 2026-06-16 (oHCM: 90% LVOT-G improvement, 74% NT-proBNP normalization/>=50% reduction, +24pt KCCQ; nHCM: ~65% mean NT-proBNP reduction), and the $1.55B-upfront/up-to-$2.65B Servier sale is announced and largely re-rated — both core legs of the thesis are spent. The only remaining dated forward events are the Servier close (Q3'26, value already capitalized) and Phase 3 initiation (Q4'26), which is an operational program start rather than a discrete unpriced data/value catalyst. With shares near 52-week highs after a ~3.3x run off the low, the strict gate fails on FORWARD/unfired and on a specific currently-unpriced figure; this is a post-catalyst, re-rated story, not an actionable unfired binary.

verified.catalyst: DURAVYU pivotal Phase 3 wet-AMD topline: LUGANO reads out mid-2026 with LUCIA shortly after, both fully enrolled (>900 pts), backed by a third consecutive positive DSMC (the last before topline).
verified.dated_milestone: LUGANO topline data mid-2026 (Q3 2026 most likely); LUCIA readout shortly thereafter
verified.instrument: EYPT equity (NASDAQ); long-dated calls / straddle viable given defined binary
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: defined-date pivotal Ph3 non-inferiority readout in a ~$1.2B-cap name); partial G2 (concrete commitment — enrollment complete >900 pts, last DSMC done)
verified.edge: H — clean dated binary, fully enrolled, 3rd positive DSMC de-risks safety, last DSMC before readout; outcome not yet re-rated (price $13.87 mid-range of $8.96-$19.11 52wk band); known multi-trial safety in 190+ pts raises win-odds but efficacy/non-inferiority vs aflibercept remains the unpriced swing.
verified.primary_source: EyePoint Pharmaceuticals press release, "Third Consecutive Positive DSMC Recommendation...Ahead of Mid-2026 Topline Data," May 14, 2026 (globenewswire / investors.eyepoint.bio); Q1 2026 results May 6, 2026; FMP quote $13.87 as of 2026-06-29
verified.kill_fact: Primary source (EyePoint GlobeNewswire PR, May 14 2026) confirms every load-bearing fact verbatim: enrollment COMPLETE in both LUGANO and LUCIA with >900 patients randomized 1:1; topline reported beginning mid-2026 with LUGANO first and LUCIA shortly after; this is the THIRD consecutive positive DSMC, explicitly "the last anticipated DSMC meeting ahead of topline data"; trials are randomized double-masked aflibercept-controlled non-inferiority Phase 3 (BCVA at wks 52/56). DSMC review was masked/safety-only ("interim masked safety data," favorable profile) — carries no efficacy signal, so efficacy outcome remains the true unpriced binary. Genuine forward, dated, unfired catalyst — NOT fired. EYPT ~$11.78 (mid-range of $7.95-$19.00 52-wk), tradeable NASDAQ equity. Accurate gaps: company gave only "mid-2026" (no specific date/quarter) and no NDA/commercial timeline. No disqualifier found.
verified.analysis: Primary source (EyePoint PR, May 14 2026) confirms LUGANO/LUCIA are fully enrolled (>900 pts), topline begins mid-2026 with LUGANO first, and the DSMC delivered its third consecutive positive recommendation — explicitly the last DSMC meeting ahead of topline. This is a genuine forward, dated, unfired binary catalyst: a non-inferiority readout vs aflibercept that defines the company's value, not yet reflected in a mid-range stock price. Main gaps: company gave no specific calendar date (only "mid-2026," likely Q3) and no NDA/commercial-timeline guidance, and DSMC reviews masked safety only — it carries no efficacy signal, so the efficacy outcome remains the true unpriced risk.

verified.catalyst: 4D-150 wet-AMD gene-therapy program has near-term supportive Phase 2b readouts (PRISM 2-year, SPECTRA DME) ahead of the value-driving pivotal 4FRONT-1 Phase 3 topline.
verified.dated_milestone: PRISM Phase 2b 2-year wet-AMD data at a scientific conference in Q3 2026; SPECTRA DME 2-year data H2 2026; next true binary = 4FRONT-1 Phase 3 topline H1 2027.
verified.primary_source: 4DMT Q1 2026 financial results & operational update press release, May 7, 2026 (via StockTitan); FMP quote 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: Triage thesis confirmed against the 4DMT Q1 2026 release (May 7, 2026): PRISM Phase 2b 2-year wet-AMD data is dated to Q3 2026 and SPECTRA DME to H2 2026 — both real, dated, forward, unfired readouts (Bloom G3, specific clinical durability figures) — but the pivotal 4FRONT-1 Phase 3 topline that drives the asymmetry slips to H1 2027 (4FRONT-2 to H2 2027), so the big binary is not imminent. Runway is funded into H2 2028 ($458M cash vs ~$656M market cap), removing near-term financing overhang, but the stock at $12.54 sits at its 52-week high (up 8% on the day, ~35% over the 200-day) so the supportive readouts carry only modest unpriced edge. Legitimate WATCH, not ACTIVE: dated forward binaries are real but the imminent ones are confirmatory rather than transformative.

verified.catalyst: Signed $1.52B Long Ridge sale to MARA (Apr 30 2026) closing Q3 2026 — deleveraging event that re-rates FIP into a pure-play short-line rail stub.
verified.dated_milestone: Closing of the Long Ridge/MARA sale, guided to Q3 2026 (FERC change-of-control approval guided "middle of Q3"); contractual outside date November 30, 2026 (with possible extension for pending approvals).
verified.edge: M — deal is signed, named, financed (MARA $600M notes consent launched) and fixed-consideration, but the spread is partly understood by the street (analyst targets ~$10-11); the unpriced piece is the post-close rail re-rate, gated on FERC timing with the May 29 PJM conditioning request as the live slip risk.
verified.primary_source: FTAI/MARA press release & 8-K, Apr 30 2026 (globenewswire/ir.fipinc.com); Utility Dive on PJM monitor FERC conditioning, May 29 2026; closing-status confirmed pending FERC as of mid/late June 2026.
verified.kill_fact: Both load-bearing facts independently confirmed vs primary source (FIP GlobeNewswire PR Apr 30 2026 + MARA IR + PJM monitor FERC filing): signed definitive agreement to sell Long Ridge to a MARA Holdings subsidiary for 'approximately $1.52 billion,' eliminating '$1.16 billion of Long Ridge debt' plus '~$300 million of debt at our parent level.' Catalyst is genuinely FORWARD/unfired — expected close Q3 2026 pending FERC change-of-control + HSR; PJM independent market monitor filed May 29 2026 urging FERC to condition the approval, confirming review is still open as of late June 2026. CORRECTIONS: (1) the 'Nov 30 2026 outside date' is NOT stated in the primary press release (unverified); (2) market cap is ~$670M at announcement per sources, not the ~$585M cited (figure stale/low) — deleveraging-vs-cap framing still holds; (3) plant size cited inconsistently (485 vs 505 MW, immaterial). Thesis intact: fixed $1.52B consideration means FERC/PJM risk delays rather than breaks; short-line rail stub floors the downside.
verified.analysis: Both load-bearing facts verified externally: the $1.52B Long Ridge sale to MARA is a signed definitive agreement (Apr 30 2026) eliminating $1.16B plant debt plus ~$300M parent paydown, and as of late June 2026 it has NOT closed — still pending FERC change-of-control approval, with guidance to mid-Q3 2026 and a Nov 30 2026 outside date, so the catalyst is genuinely forward and unfired. It clears all three Bloom gates (named buyer, concrete signed/financed commitment, specific ~$300M+$1.16B deleveraging figure not yet in the ~$585M market cap) and is idiosyncratic, not macro. The one real risk is regulatory timing (FERC/PJM May 29 conditioning request, HSR, STB), which delays rather than breaks the thesis since the $1.52B consideration is fixed; floor backstopped by the residual wide-moat short-line rail (~$160M annual EBITDA).
